title: Bitter Creamy German Cucumber En-Croûte
description: A lacto-vegetarian-friendly German dish featuring a Persian cucumber filling, wrapped in a buttery, flaky pastry crust, perfect for a festive dinner or special occasion. The combination of creamy sauce, pickled cucumbers, and nutty mustard creates a unique, palate-cleansing flavor experience.

ingredients.name: Dijon mustard
ingredients.notes: use wholegrain mustard for a coarser texture

instructions: Place the flour, salt, and cold butter in a mixing bowl, and use a pastry blender to work the butter into the flour until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add the ice water, stirring with a fork until the dough just comes together in a ball.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead a few times until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Roll out the chilled dough on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch (3 mm).
Transfer the dough to a 9x13-inch baking dish and trim the edges to fit.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the mustard, cream, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Add the pickled mustard seeds and stir to combine.
Arrange the cucumbers in a single layer in the dough-lined baking dish.
Spread the mustard-cream mixture over the cucumbers, leaving a 1-inch (2.5 cm) border around the edges.
Sprinkle the cheese over the top of the filling.
Fold the edges of the dough up over the filling, pressing gently to seal.
Cut a few slits in the top crust to allow steam to escape during baking.
Brush the egg wash over the top of the crust.
Bake for 35-40 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the filling is heated through.
Let the en-croûte rest for 10-15 minutes before serving
